IT security team - security assessments of OSPs
Compliance department - OSP assessment records
Internal audit - OSP audit reports

auditTips: AI Generated: Request a complete list of all OSPs with access to customer information and check whether due diligence has been conducted for each one.
AI Generated: Compare the FSP's own control standards with what it requires from its OSPs - paragraph 12.3 requires comparable standards, so a significant gap is a finding.
AI Generated: Check whether due diligence covers customer information disposal practices, not just processing and storage.

12.4.title: OSP Service Level Agreement Requirements
12.4.auditorFocus: AI Generated: BNM examiners will review OSP SLAs against the nine mandatory requirements specified in paragraph 12.4. This is a granular compliance check - examiners will verify each of the nine elements is present in the SLA. A key examiner focus is whether existing SLAs have been amended to comply (per footnote 24, which requires immediate amendment, not just upon renewal). Examiners will also check whether SLA provisions are actually enforced and monitored.

evidenceItems.name: OSP SLA Compliance Assessment
evidenceItems.description: Assessment of OSP SLAs against the nine mandatory requirements of paragraph 12.4, including evidence that existing SLAs have been amended to comply.
whatGoodLooksLike: All nine mandatory SLA elements are present in each OSP agreement
Existing SLAs have been amended to comply, not deferred to renewal
SLA includes agreed timeframe for breach reporting to the FSP
Data return or destruction obligations upon SLA termination are specified
FSP audit rights over OSP customer information handling are contractually established
Personnel vetting requirements are specified
commonGaps: One or more of the nine mandatory elements is missing from OSP SLAs
Existing SLAs have not been amended - compliance is deferred to next renewal
Breach reporting timeframe is not specified or is unreasonably long
Data destruction or return upon termination is mentioned generally but lacks specifics (e.g., reference to paragraph 10.32 destruction standards)
FSP audit rights are included but have never been exercised
suggestedSources: Legal department - SLA templates and executed agreements
Vendor management team - SLA compliance register
Compliance department - SLA gap analysis
Procurement department - SLA amendment records
evidenceItems.format: PDF SLAs and compliance gap analysis

auditTips: AI Generated: Create a nine-point checklist from paragraph 12.4(a)-(i) and map each OSP SLA against it. Many FSPs will be missing at least one element.
AI Generated: Pay special attention to footnote 24 which requires existing SLAs to be amended immediately, not upon renewal. Check when SLAs were last amended relative to MCIPD effective date.
AI Generated: Verify that audit rights (12.4(i)) have been exercised at least once - contractual rights that are never used provide no assurance.

12.6-12.9.title: OSP NDA, Training, and Biennial Review
12.6-12.9.auditorFocus: AI Generated: BNM examiners will verify: (1) binding non-disclosure undertakings with all OSPs handling customer information, (2) OSP personnel training on customer information handling at regular intervals with effectiveness reviews, and (3) biennial reviews of OSPs confirming contractual compliance. Examiners will sample NDAs, training records, and review reports across the OSP portfolio.

evidenceItems.name: OSP Non-Disclosure Undertakings
evidenceItems.description: Executed binding non-disclosure agreements with all OSPs handling customer information.
whatGoodLooksLike: Binding NDA executed with every OSP that handles customer information
NDA specifically covers customer information handling obligations
NDA survives contract termination
Complete inventory of NDAs maintained and tracked
commonGaps: Not all OSPs have signed NDAs
NDAs are generic and do not specifically address customer information
NDA inventory is incomplete - no tracking of which OSPs have signed
Legacy OSPs were never asked to sign NDAs
suggestedSources: Legal department - executed NDA register
Vendor management team - OSP documentation files
Procurement department - contract management system

evidenceItems.name: OSP Personnel Training Records and Effectiveness Review
evidenceItems.description: Evidence that OSPs provide regular training to their personnel on customer information handling and that training effectiveness is reviewed.
whatGoodLooksLike: OSP training records showing regular training intervals (at least annual)
Training content covers relevant customer information handling policies and procedures
FSP reviews or receives reports on OSP training adequacy and effectiveness
FSP may provide training directly to OSP personnel (per 12.8 guidance)
commonGaps: FSP relies on contractual requirement for OSP training but never verifies compliance
No records of OSP personnel training are obtained or reviewed
Training effectiveness is not assessed by either the FSP or the OSP
OSP training content does not cover the FSP's specific customer information requirements
suggestedSources: Vendor management team - OSP training attestations
OSP relationship managers - training review records
Compliance department - OSP training compliance reports

evidenceItems.name: Biennial OSP Review Reports
evidenceItems.description: Reports from biennial reviews of OSPs confirming compliance with contractual obligations for safeguarding customer information.
whatGoodLooksLike: Formal review conducted at least once every two years for each OSP
Review scope covers all contractual obligations for customer information safeguarding
Review includes assessment of OSP controls, not just SLA performance metrics
Findings documented with remediation actions tracked
Review findings reported to senior management
commonGaps: No biennial review conducted - reliance on SLA monitoring alone
Review is limited to SLA performance metrics without assessing customer information controls
Review cycle has lapsed beyond two years for some OSPs
Findings are not documented or tracked to remediation
suggestedSources: Vendor management team - OSP review reports
Internal audit - OSP audit reports
Compliance department - OSP compliance review records

auditTips: AI Generated: Cross-reference the OSP register with the NDA register to identify any OSPs handling customer information without an executed NDA.
AI Generated: Request OSP training evidence for the last two years - if the FSP cannot produce it, the contractual requirement under 12.7 is not being monitored.
AI Generated: Check the biennial review schedule against actual review dates for each OSP - overdue reviews are a direct compliance gap under 12.9.

12.10.title: OSP Customer Information Sharing Records
12.10.auditorFocus: AI Generated: BNM examiners will verify that FSPs maintain accurate and complete records and audit trails of all customer information shared with or given to OSPs. Examiners will test whether the records are current, complete, and can be used to identify exactly what customer information each OSP has access to.

evidenceItems.name: Customer Information Sharing Records with OSPs
evidenceItems.description: Records and audit trails of all customer information shared with or provided to outsourced service providers.
whatGoodLooksLike: Comprehensive records showing what customer information has been shared with each OSP
Records include type of information, volume, purpose, date of sharing, and method of transfer
Audit trails are maintained for ongoing data access by OSPs
Records are accurate and regularly reconciled
Records support the ability to identify all data at risk if an OSP experiences a breach
commonGaps: No centralised record of customer information shared with OSPs
Records are incomplete - some data transfers are not documented
Audit trails for ongoing OSP access are not maintained
Records have not been updated when new data types are shared with existing OSPs
suggestedSources: Vendor management team - data sharing records
IT operations - data transfer logs
Data governance team - data sharing inventory
Compliance department - data sharing register

auditTips: AI Generated: Ask the FSP to identify exactly what customer information a specific OSP has access to - inability to answer this question precisely indicates inadequate records under 12.10.
AI Generated: Compare the data sharing records against actual data flows by sampling API logs or data transfer mechanisms to verify completeness of records.

13.1-13.3.section: Conditions in relation to permitted disclosure
13.1-13.3.title: Permitted Disclosure Controls
13.1-13.3.auditorFocus: AI Generated: BNM examiners will verify that adequate control measures are in place over permitted disclosures of customer information under the FSA, IFSA, and DFIA schedules. The three minimum control areas are: (a) verification processes for authenticity of disclosure orders/requests, (b) documentation requirements, and (c) approval authority levels at an appropriately senior level. Examiners will sample recent disclosure requests and trace the control process from request receipt to approval to disclosure.

evidenceItems.name: Permitted Disclosure Control Framework
evidenceItems.description: Control framework governing permitted disclosures of customer information, including verification procedures, documentation requirements, and approval authority levels.
whatGoodLooksLike: Documented procedures covering all categories of permitted disclosure under Schedule 11 FSA/IFSA and Fourth Schedule DFIA
Verification process for confirming authenticity of disclosure orders and requests
Documentation requirements specifying what records must be maintained for each disclosure
Authority levels for approving disclosures set at an appropriately senior level
Register of all permitted disclosures made
commonGaps: No formal verification process for disclosure request authenticity
Approval authority is not defined or is set too low
Documentation of disclosures made is incomplete or inconsistent
Not all categories of permitted disclosure are covered by the control framework
No register or log of disclosures made
suggestedSources: Legal department - permitted disclosure procedures and register
Compliance department - disclosure control framework
Operations team - disclosure processing records
Board Secretariat - authority level approvals

auditTips: AI Generated: Sample 5-10 recent permitted disclosures and trace the full control process: was the request verified for authenticity, was it documented, and was it approved at an appropriately senior level?
AI Generated: Check whether the authority levels for approving disclosures are formally defined and whether approvals are actually obtained at the defined level in practice.
AI Generated: Verify that the FSP distinguishes between different types of permitted disclosure (court orders, regulatory requests, group sharing, etc.) with appropriate controls for each.

13.4.section: Conditions in relation to permitted disclosure
13.4.title: BNM Approval Application for Disclosure
13.4.auditorFocus: AI Generated: BNM examiners will verify that the FSP is aware of the requirement to apply to BNM using Appendix V for disclosures under section 134(1)(b) FSA, section 146(1)(b) IFSA, or section 120(1)(b) DFIA, and that any such applications have been properly completed and submitted.

evidenceItems.name: BNM Disclosure Approval Applications
evidenceItems.description: Records of applications submitted to BNM for customer information disclosure approval using Appendix V, including application forms and BNM responses.
whatGoodLooksLike: Appendix V application form properly completed for each applicable disclosure
Application submitted to BNM before disclosure is made
BNM approval obtained and on file before disclosure proceeds
Register of all Appendix V applications and their status
commonGaps: FSP is unaware of the Appendix V requirement
Disclosures were made without first obtaining BNM approval
Application forms are incomplete or improperly completed
No register of applications and their outcomes
suggestedSources: Legal department - Appendix V applications and BNM correspondence
Compliance department - disclosure approval register
Board Secretariat - records of disclosure approval requests
evidenceItems.format: PDF application forms and BNM approval letters

auditTips: AI Generated: Ask the FSP whether any disclosures have been made under section 134(1)(b) FSA, 146(1)(b) IFSA, or 120(1)(b) DFIA and verify that Appendix V was completed and BNM approval obtained before each disclosure.
AI Generated: Check whether the compliance team is aware of the Appendix V process - lack of awareness indicates a gap even if no applicable disclosures have been made.
